rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 1, PETITIONER NIGEL CHANTER, LAI CHEUNG, PETER BAUMANNN, ALISON BAUMANN, TON-YUN FANG, GNOMES COVE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, BABAC DOANE's Motion To Compel Judgment Debtor Gregory Malins Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) And To Compel Compliance With Responses To Document Requests. Continued to April 25, 2025, on the court's motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ).

rulings.Case Title: DAPHNE WANG VS. BRIGHT IDEA INCORPORATED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents To Defendant Bright Idea Incorporated, No. 21; And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 11, PLAINTIFF DAPHNE WANG's, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents To Defendant Bright Idea Incorporated, No. 21; And Production Of Documents. Plaintiff Daphne Wang's motion to compel further responses from Defendant Bright Idea Incorporated to her Request for Production of Documents, No. 21 and for production of documents in accordance with Defendant's statement of compliance with respect to Request for Production of Documents, Nos. 27 and 28 is GRANTED. Plaintiff's meet and confer was reasonable. The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown Defendant Bright Idea Incorporated responses are incomplete. Defendant Bright Idea Incorporated has not demonstrated that any of its objections has merit. The court orders Defendant Bright Idea Incorporated to provide further responses to Request for Production Nos. 21 and include all recordings of Bright Idea's Friday work meetings during Plaintiff's employment. Defendant Bright Idea Incorporated appears to concede it has failed to produce documents in accordance with its statement of compliance with respect to Request for Production Of Documents Nos. 27 and 28. In any event, the court orders Defendant to produce documents in accordance with its statement of compliance for Nos. 27 and 28. Defendant Bright Idea Incorporated must provide a verification. For the responses to which Defendant Bright Idea Incorporated is claiming a privilege, Defendant shall produce a privilege log. Further responses, verification (if not yet provided) and privilege log due no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. The court does not find Defendant Bright Idea Incorporated acted with substantial justification. Defendant shall pay $500 as sanctions, payable to the court by way of the clerk of the court (a copy of this order to accompany payment), payment no later than by April 28, 2025, receipt filed no later than May 4, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL WARD VS. LONNIE GEORGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 7, DEFENDANT LONNIE GEORGE, AX9 SECURITY, INC.'s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Cross-Complaint. Defendant Lonnie George's unopposed Motion for Leave to File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED. Leave to file a cross-complaint "may be granted in the interest of justice at any time during the course of the action." (CCP Section 428.50(c).) "A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99.) Good cause appearing, Defendant is granted leave to file the proposed cross-complaint. Defendant must file his cross-complaint, substantially identical to Exhibit A to the Choo Declaration, within ten days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: ADEENA ELLISON VS. JEWISH LEARNINGWORKS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Without Prejudice (Paga)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 8, PLAINTIFF ADEENA ELLISON's Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Without Prejudice. Adeena Ellison's unopposed Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Without Prejudice (PAGA) is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Plaintiff fails to identify a statutory or common law basis for her motion. Plaintiff must identify the legal basis for her request, the applicable legal basis for adjudication and an analysis thereon. If the motion is made under Code of Civil Procedure section 581(b)(1), the court will not authorize dismissal absent more information, in light of the fact that dismissal under this statute generally does not require a court order. If the motion is Labor Code section 2699(s), the court needs much more information and analysis. "The superior court shall review and approve any settlement of any civil action filed pursuant to this part." (Lab. Code, section 2699(s)(2).) Here, Plaintiff, who accepted the role of public prosecutor for purposes of the representative claim(s), must (at the very least) submit the settlement agreement for the individual claim(s) and explain how dismissal of the representative claims is in the public interest. Section 2699(s)(2) establishes another precondition to judicial approval of a PAGA settlement: "The proposed settlement shall be submitted to the agency at the same time that it is submitted to the court." This request for dismissal of the representative claim(s) is based on settlement of Plaintiff's individual claim. Plaintiff here has not demonstrated that she submitted her papers to the LWDA. It is unlikely the court will approve dismissal of the representative claims on these grounds on a subsequent motion absent notice, and proof thereof, to the LWDA. Notice should be provided consistent with Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b). Plaintiff is ordered to submit by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order consistent with this tentative forthwith. The court's order on this motion must be references and included with any subsequent motion seeking substantially similar relief. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: MARTE J. BASSI VS. JOSEPH B. ADAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: CROSS DEFENDANT MARTE BASSI, W. EDLIN, FRED BLUM'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT MARTE BASSI, W. EDLIN, FRED BLUM's Motion To Compel Arbitration. (Part 2 of 2). Cross-defendants' notice of motion seeks a stay of "further proceedings" and their points and authorities apparently only seeks a stay of prosecution of the cross-complaint. Generally, when the court issues a stay, it stays the "action." (CCP 1281.4.) As explained above, a stay of the entire action is the most efficient manner to resolve the parties' disputes. Lastly, cross-complainant fails to establish that cross-defendants waived the right to arbitrate by their silence and other conduct. (Quach v. California Commerce Club, Inc. (2024) 16 Cal.5th 562, ["To establish waiver under generally applicable contract law, the party opposing enforcement of a contractual agreement must prove by clear and convincing evidence that the waiving party knew of the contractual right and intentionally relinquished or abandoned it."].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: MARTE J. BASSI VS. JOSEPH B. ADAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2025, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT MARTE BASSI, W. EDLIN, FRED BLUM's Motion To Compel Arbitration. (Part 1 of 2). Cross-defendants Bassi, Edlin, and Blum's motion to compel arbitration and stay is GRANTED. "The petitioner bears the burden of proving the existence of a valid arbitration agreement by the preponderance of the evidence, and a party opposing the petition bears the burden of proving by a preponderance of the evidence any fact necessary to its defense." (Engalla v. Permanente Medical Group, Inc. (1997) 15 Cal.4th 951, 972.) In Gamboa v. Northeast Community Clinic (2021) 72 Cal.App.5th 158, the court describes the three-step process to establish the existence of an agreement to arbitrate. "First, the moving party bears the burden of producing 'prima facie evidence of a written agreement to arbitrate the controversy.'" (Gamboa, 72 Cal.App.5th at 165.) For this step, "it is not necessary to follow the normal procedures of document authentication." (Id.; Condee v. Longwood Management Corp. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 215, 218 [same].) Indeed, the party seeking arbitration "need only allege the existence of an agreement and support the allegation as provided in rule [3.1330]." (Iyere v. Wise Auto Group (2023) 87 Cal.App.5th 747, 755, quoting Condee v. Longwood Management Corp. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 215, 218.) Here, Cross-defendants demonstrate the existence of an enforceable arbitration agreement. Section H.19(b) of the parties' Amended Partnership Agreement states that valuation of partnership units "shall be submitted to binding baseball arbitration." (Cross-complaint, Ex. C.) Section K.2 of that agreement further states that the parties agreed to arbitrate "[a]ny controversy or claim arising out of, or relating in any manner to, this AMENDED AGREEMENT, or any breach hereof" (Id.) Here, causes of action 2 [breach of partnership agreement], 3 [breach of fiduciary duty], 4 [fraud], 5 [accounting], and 6 [interference with contract] in the cross-complaint implicate the arbitration agreement and are subject to arbitration. Cause of action 1 [breach of 2015 promissory note] is expressly exempted from arbitration. (Cross-complaint, Ex. B pg. 2.) Given the interplay between the various promissory notes (not subject to arbitration) and the valuation of the partnership shares (subject to arbitration), there is a possibility of conflicting rulings. The court determines that the promissory note claims in the complaint and cross-complaint are not severable per CCP 1281.4 (such that proceedings should and can reasonably proceed both in the court and in arbitration simultaneously) and the entire action should be stayed. (Continued to Part 2).
